Each document coins and consistently uses its own terms within that document only; currently the implemented behavior for T3 and T4. For reasoning-era, information-bearing text this is superseded by the persistent world-state (node 36).

Definitions and descriptions invent terms and keep them coherent inside a single document, which is cheap and needs no shared vocabulary. Consistency across documents requires the persistent world-state generator (node 36), not the deferred S3 rung as originally scoped.

Local consistency gives definitions and descriptions internal coherence without the machinery of a corpus-wide invented lexicon. A document may introduce terms and use them consistently within itself; consistency is not required across documents in this mechanism. It remains the current implemented behavior, but it cannot by itself produce a corpus that is both information-bearing and internally consistent across documents (node 34); that requires promoting coordination to a persistent, program-held world-state (node 36), which this mechanism is superseded by for any reasoning-era or cross-document text.
